'''''Teljkon Vagabond''''' was the name given to a 1,500-[[Meter/Legends|meter]] organic [[Starship/Legends|starship]] created by the [[Qella]] to house their [[Species/Legends|species]]' culture and history when their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Brath Qella]] plunged into a sudden ice age. The vessel's ultimate goal was to return to Brath Qella, use its energy beams to thaw the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], and restart its evolutionary process. It acquired its name after a sighting in the [[Teljkon system/Legends|Teljkon system]].

==Characteristics==
The ship was equipped with a weapon called the [[EM Whip]]. It shot two large spikes on chains into an enemy vessel and then ran very high-voltage electric current through the now-closed circuit. This knocked out all electronics and [[Death/Legends|killed]] or knocked out all enemy crew members. It was only effective at short range because of the physical space requirement of the cables.

The vessel's other weapon was termed the [[Scythe Weapon]]. The scythe consisted of three beams emitted from different locations on the ''Vagabond'''s hull (after being sent through a complex internal superconducting network) which then scythed together (hence the name) to destroy any point that the three intersected on. It had an enormous effective range of at least 3,500 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]], but was inefficient in terms of fleet combat, as it could only focus on three or four targets at a time and only one such system could be equipped to any [[Starship/Legends|starship]].

The ''Vagabond'' had what seemed to be organic walls and bulkheads, and the ship's walls would fix themselves after being damaged.
[[File:TeljkonVagabondExploration-TEC.jpg|thumb|left|210px|[[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando]], [[Lobot/Legends|Lobot]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] exploring the Vagabond's weapon's superconduit network.]]

==History==
[[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], [[Lobot/Legends|Lobot]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] managed to board the ''Vagabond'' in [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]] as part of a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] effort to capture it. They eventually worked out its true purpose. Lando and Lobot discovered the vessel's organic nature when trying to escape from the ships airlock after the ship jumped to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] after damaging the [[Agave-class picket ship|interdiction picket]] ''[[Kauri]]'' with its "scythe" attack. The ''Vagabond'' ended up in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] world of [[Prakith/Legends|Prakith]] located in [[Sector 5/Legends|Sector 5]]. The ''Vagabond'' was attacked by the Imperial patrol frigate ''[[Bloodprice]]'', the ''Vagabond'' then attacked back with its "mace" attack. ''Bloodprice'' was destroyed including all hands aboard. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Strike-class medium cruiser|cruiser]] ''[[Gorath]]'' witnessed the attack and notified the Imperial [[Adz-class patrol destroyer|''Adz''-class patrol destroyer]] ''[[Tobay]]'' to prepare for a [[Precision hyperspace jump|micro*jump]] to help ''Gorath'' intercept the ''TeLjkon Vagabond''. ''Gorath'' was destroyed by the ''Vagabond'' but ''Tobay'' survived ironically since it was older than ''Gorath'' and had many problems with its sensor and navigational equipment.
